homepage.title: Homepage | Rt Hon Sir Andrew Mitchell MP
homepage.h1: Member of Parliament for Royal Sutton Coldfield since 2001
homepage.text_excerpt: Homepage | Rt Hon Sir Andrew Mitchell MP Skip to main content Main navigation Home About Andrew News In Parliament Royal Sutton Coldfield Gallery Contact Visiting Parliament What your MP can do for you Toggle navigation Main navigation Home About Andrew News In Parliament Royal Sutton Coldfield Gallery Contact Member of Parliament for Royal Sutton Coldfield since 2001 Former Deputy Foreign Secretary and Shadow Foreign Secretary. It is my pleasure and privilege to serve the people of Royal Sutton Coldfield . I always work hard to help local residents and to champion the issues of importance to our town. I hope that this website provides you with a useful insight into some of the work I do on behalf of my constituents – both in Sutton Coldfield and in the House of Commons. Over the year thousands of local residents contact me by letter, email and in person. If I can be of any help to you, please do not hesitate to get in touch. You can also follow me over on my Facebook page here . Rt Hon Sir Andrew Mitchell MP In Pictures Proud to cut the ribbon and officially open Harvey Norman 24 April 2026 Today local MP Sir Andrew Mitchell and former West Midlands Mayor Sir Andy Street officially opened the new Harvey Norman store in our town centre. Backing Sutton United to win promotion 24 April 2026 Yesterday Sir Andrew Mitchell visited Sutton United Football Club in Coleshill Road alongside Reddicap councillor Richard Parkin, ahead of the club’s much anticipated play-off semi-final this weekend at home against Heather St Johns FC. Tackling offroad bike menace 17 April 2026 Sir Andrew Mitchell recently welcomed local resident Lee Bashford to his advice surgery who has raised concerns about off-road motorbikes on The Greenway and surrounding area. Weekly Message Weekly Message to Constituents 315 - A new era for our town centre 24 April 2026 Today it was my great pleasure to join our former West Midlands Mayor Sir Andy Street to officially open the new Harvey Norman store in our town centre. Weekly Message to Constituents 314 - Frustration as development gets go-ahead despite campaign 17 April 2026 Unwanted Wishaw battery plan gets the green light despite community opposition. Weekly Message to Constituents 313 - A welcome boost for local healthcare 10 April 2026 This week I had a wonderful time officially opening the brand new Vesey Pharmacy at Ley Hill Surgery – a welcome addition to Sutton Coldfield’s healthcare services. All News In Parliament Sir Andrew Mitchell pushes for a clear national accident prevention strategy 28 April 2026 Sir Andrew Mitchell leads a Westminster Hall debate on the importance of developing a clear and comprehensive national accident prevention strategy. Sir Andrew Mitchell presses Government to get tough on overseas territories over beneficial ownership registers 21 April 2026 Sir Andrew Mitchell calls on the Government to end the delay and take a tougher stance against those overseas territories that still have not established publicly accessible registers of beneficial ownership as required under the Sanctions and Anti-Money Laundering Act 2018. Sir Andrew Mitchell calls out PM for shifting blame onto Sir Olly Robbins in Mandelson vetting scandal 20 April 2026 Former Deputy Foreign Secretary Sir Andrew Mitchell challenges the Prime Minister for attempting to shift responsibility onto senior civil servant Sir Olly Robbins for the vetting controversy surrounding Peter Mandelson’s appointment as UK Ambassador to the United States. All Parliament News Map Posts from X The Kigali Declaration Andrew has a keen interest in international development and in 2007 founded Project Umubano, the Conservative Party’s ten-year social action project in Rwanda. The project culminated with the Kigali Declaration against Genocide and Identity-based Violence. pages.title: About Andrew | Rt Hon Sir Andrew Mitchell MP
pages.text_excerpt: About Andrew | Rt Hon Sir Andrew Mitchell MP Skip to main content Main navigation Home About Andrew News In Parliament Royal Sutton Coldfield Gallery Contact Visiting Parliament What your MP can do for you Toggle navigation Main navigation Home About Andrew News In Parliament Royal Sutton Coldfield Gallery Contact Sir Andrew Mitchell Sir Andrew was born in 1956 and is married with two daughters. He was educated at Rugby School and Cambridge University, where he studied history and was elected as President of the Cambridge Union in 1978. Andrew served in the Army (Royal Tank Regiment) before joining Lazard where he worked with British companies seeking large-scale overseas contracts. He was the Member of Parliament for Gedling from 1987 to 1997. During this period he held office as a Government Whip and as Minister for Social Security. He also served as a Vice-Chairman of the Conservative Party from 1992 to 1993. In 2001 he was re-elected to Parliament as MP for Sutton Coldfield. In November 2003, he was appointed Shadow Minister for Economic Affairs. From September 2004 until the end of the Parliamentary term, he was Shadow Minister for Home Affairs. Following the General Election in May 2005 Andrew joined the Shadow Cabinet and was appointed Shadow Secretary of State for International Development. Andrew Mitchell was Secretary of State for International Development from May 2010 until September 2012 and Government Chief Whip from September – October 2012. Andrew returned to the Government serving as Minister of State in the Foreign, Commonwealth & Development Office from October 2022 to July 2024. In April 2024 he was given the title of Deputy Foreign Secretary. Andrew served as Shadow Foreign Secretary from July to November 2024. An experienced and highly effective MP, Andrew is committed to serving the people of Sutton Coldfield. Andrew is currently campaigning on a number of important local issues in Sutton Coldfield including issues affecting our local environment and the general well-being of the Sutton Coldfield community. He is very active in addressing issues of local development where he feels they adversely affect the Town. He actively supports a number of local charities including Breastfriends, Sutton Coldfield Branch of the RNLI, Parkinson’s Disease Society, Sutton Coldfield Sea Cadets, Greenacres, and Sutton Coldfield Guiding. Andrew is also a Parliamentary Disabled Children’s Champion for the Disabled Children’s Partnership. Since becoming Sutton Coldfield’s MP he is particularly pleased to have achieved the reinstatement of the Sutton Coldfield Civic Service and established the Sutton Coldfield Inter-Schools Debating Competition. As Sutton Coldfield’s MP, Andrew deals with hundreds of letters and emails from constituents every week. He has a dedicated staff of 5 people to assist him with this work. The issues raised are wide ranging and can fall within the responsibility of Government Departments or Birmingham City Council as well as a number of the agencies that now administer specific matters such as immigration, benefits and the environment. In addition he holds regular Advice Sessions where constituents can see him to discuss political issues or matters of personal concern. Andrew has a home in Sutton Coldfield and spends as much time as possible regularly visiting local schools, businesses and voluntary organisations. Rt Hon Sir Andrew Mitchell MP House of Commons London SW1A 0AA Westminster Office: 020 7219 8516 Constituency Office: 0121 354 2229 Email: andrew.mitchell.mp@parliament.uk Sutton Coldfield Constituency: Sutton Four Oaks, Sutton Mere Green, Sutton Reddicap, Sutton Roughley, Sutton Trinity, Sutton Vesey, Sutton Walmley & Minworth, Sutton Wylde Green. In Parliament Image Sir Andrew Mitchell pushes for a clear national accident prevention strategy 28 April 2026 Sir Andrew Mitchell leads a Westminster Hall debate on the importance of developing a clear and comprehensive national accident prevention strategy. Image Sir Andrew Mitchell presses Government to get tough on overseas territories over beneficial ownership registers 21 April 2026 Sir Andrew Mitchell calls on the Government to end the delay and take a tougher stance against those overseas territories that still have not established publicly accessible registers of beneficial ownership as required under the Sanctions and Anti-Money Laundering Act 2018. Image Sir Andrew Mitchell calls out PM for shifting blame onto Sir Olly Robbins in Mandelson vetting scandal 20 April 2026 Former Deputy Foreign Secretary Sir Andrew Mitchell challenges the Prime Minister for attempting to shift responsibility onto senior civil servant Sir Olly Robbins for the vetting controversy surrounding Peter Mandelson’s appointment as UK Ambassador to the United States. Image Sir Andrew Mitchell calls for immediate uplift in defence spending 13 April 2026 Former Deputy Foreign Secretary Sir Andrew Mitchell urges the PM to commit to an immediate uplift in defence spending towards a 5% increase so that the UK can play a full part in European defence. Image Sir Andrew Mitchell criticises PM’s “anaemic” response to US strikes as out of step with Five Eyes 2 March 2026 Former Deputy Foreign Secretary Sir Andrew Mitchell criticises the Prime Minister's response last week as weak and disappointing, and out of step with other Five Eyes nations. He urges review of shadow Attorney General Lord Wolfson KC's analysis, which argues British active support for allied strikes is fully consistent with international law. Image Written Parliamentary Questions – February 2026 28 February 2026 Written questions allow MPs to ask for information on the work, policy and activities of Government departments, related bodies, and the administration of Parliament. Image Sir Andrew Mitchell urges Government to focus on clemency for Jimmy Lai 9 February 2026 Former Deputy Foreign Secretary Sir Andrew Mitchell calls on the Government to focus 100% on securing clemency for Jimmy Lai and a one-way ticket back to the UK and to his family. Image Sir Andrew Mitchell seeks PM’s assurance that release of Jimmy Lai was raised on China visit 2 February 2026 Former Deputy Foreign Secretary Sir Andrew Mitchell welcomes the Prime Minister’s visit to China and seeks assurances from the PM that he personally raised the case for clemency for 78-year-old British citizen Jimmy Lai who is being held in solitary confinement in Hong Kong. Image Written Parliamentary Questions – January 2026 31 January 2026 Written questions allow MPs to ask for information on the work, policy and activities of Government departments, related bodies, and the administration of Parliament. pages.title: Contact | Rt Hon Sir Andrew Mitchell MP
pages.text_excerpt: Contact | Rt Hon Sir Andrew Mitchell MP Skip to main content Main navigation Home About Andrew News In Parliament Royal Sutton Coldfield Gallery Contact Visiting Parliament What your MP can do for you Toggle navigation Main navigation Home About Andrew News In Parliament Royal Sutton Coldfield Gallery Contact Visiting Parliament What your MP can do for you Contact Sir Andrew MPs are only permitted to offer advice and handle correspondence from their own constituents, please therefore include your full address and postcode in all correspondence. For the fastest response please email andrew.mitchell.mp@parliament.uk with your full name and address, a contact telephone number and a brief outline of your query. Post: Rt Hon Sir Andrew Mitchell MP House of Commons London SW1A 0AA Telephone: Westminster Office: 020 7219 8516 Constituency Office: 0121 354 2229 Email: andrew.mitchell.mp@parliament.uk Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/andrewmitchellmp/ Protecting Your Data: My office will not use your personal data in any way that goes beyond your reasonable expectations in contacting me. Please see my Privacy Notice . Advice Surgeries: If you are a constituent and would like to arrange an advice session appointment, please email andrew.mitchell.mp@parliament.uk with ‘advice session request’ as the subject line. You will need to provide your full name, postal address and a brief summary of what you wish to discuss. Should email not be possible, please call 0207 219 1689 instead. For all other matters please email andrew.mitchell.mp@parliament.uk or call 020 7219 8516. Rt Hon Sir Andrew Mitchell MP House of Commons London SW1A 0AA Westminster Office: 020 7219 8516 Constituency Office: 0121 354 2229 Email: andrew.mitchell.mp@parliament.uk Sutton Coldfield Constituency: Sutton Four Oaks, Sutton Mere Green, Sutton Reddicap, Sutton Roughley, Sutton Trinity, Sutton Vesey, Sutton Walmley & Minworth, Sutton Wylde Green. pages.title: What your MP can do for you | Rt Hon Sir Andrew Mitchell MP
pages.text_excerpt: What your MP can do for you | Rt Hon Sir Andrew Mitchell MP Skip to main content Main navigation Home About Andrew News In Parliament Royal Sutton Coldfield Gallery Contact Visiting Parliament What your MP can do for you Toggle navigation Main navigation Home About Andrew News In Parliament Royal Sutton Coldfield Gallery Contact Visiting Parliament What your MP can do for you What your MP can do for you This section is designed to answer any questions you may have about the role of MPs and how they can help you. As the Member of Parliament for Sutton Coldfield, Andrew’s role is to represent his constituents at Westminster, regardless of whether or not they voted for him. It is important to note that Members of Parliament can only deal with issues raised by their own constituents, so make sure you check you are contacting the MP who represents your constituency. What can my MP do to help me? Andrew can help you with all matters for which Parliament or central government is responsible, such as: Tax problems involving the HM Revenue and Customs Department . Problems dealt with by the Department for Work and Pensions such as benefits, pensions and National Insurance. Problems dealt with by the Home Office , such as immigration. Problems dealt with by the Department of Health , such as hospitals and the National Health Service. Problems dealt with by the Department for Education , such as school closures and grants. How does my MP deal with my problem? Andrew can deal with constituents’ problems in a number of ways, some of which include: A letter to the relevant organisation. A letter to the relevant Government Minister. Seek clarification of the reason for decisions taken. Support appeals by individuals or communities. Many problems (but not all) can be resolved in this way. Whilst an MP cannot always obtain the answer you would like he can ascertain that correct procedures have been followed. If you feel there has been unnecessary delay or maladministration Andrew may be able to take your case to the Parliamentary Ombudsman. Individuals cannot take their case direct to the Ombudsman but must do so through an MP. When should I contact someone else? Some issues are not the direct responsibility of Parliament or government. In these instances, you should first contact either your local council or your nearest Citizens Advice Bureau before considering contacting your MP. These are issues such as: Council tax. Private problems with neighbours, landlords, employers, family; or companies who’ve sold you faulty goods. Decisions made by the courts. Issues that are the responsibility of your Local Authority, ie dustbins or street repairs. If your problem concerns the Local Authority you should contact your local Councillor. Details on all of Sutton Coldfield’s Councillors and how to contact them are available on Birmingham City Council's website. For details of all your elected representatives visit www.writetothem.com . Protecting Your Data In line with data protection regulations, my Office processes constituents’ data for casework and policy query purposes under the lawful basis of public interest. In instances where this lawful basis is not sufficient and explicit consent is required, a member of my office will contact you to establish your consent. My Office holds all data indefinitely until it is no longer relevant or necessary for us to do so. However, data will be processed only to the extent to which it is necessary to achieve the stated purposes of assisting with casework and policy matters. Please note that you hold rights in relation to our data controlling and data processing activities which is detailed on the Information Commissioner’s Office website. You can access this by clicking here . Rt Hon Sir Andrew Mitchell MP House of Commons London SW1A 0AA Westminster Office: 020 7219 8516 Constituency Office: 0121 354 2229 Email: andrew.mitchell.mp@parliament.uk Sutton Coldfield Constituency: Sutton Four Oaks, Sutton Mere Gree
